question: What is the process to upgrade an unmanaged package that is currently installed in production?
optionA: Install the new version to a Developer org then deploy to production.
optionB: Uninstall the current version and install the new version.
optionC: Click the update link on the Installed Packages page.
optionD: Use the Install Wizard to install the upgrade to production.
explanation: Unmanaged packages CANNOT be upgraded. Once installed, the only way to get a newer version of an unmanaged package into an org is to uninstall the existing version and install the new version. There is no 'update' link for unmanaged packages on the Installed Packages page — that is a feature of managed packages only. Reference: Salesforce Help — 'Unmanaged packages aren't upgradeable.'

question: An app builder would like to streamline the user experience by reflecting summarized calculations of specific fields on various objects. Which field types could be used in roll-up summary fields to accomplish this? Choose 3 answers
optionC: Date
explanation: Roll-up summary fields aggregate values from child records in a master-detail relationship. The field being summarized must be numeric or date-based. Currency, Number, and Percent fields support SUM, MIN, MAX (and COUNT). Date fields support MIN and MAX. Checkbox and Time fields cannot be used as the aggregated field in a roll-up summary. (COUNT works on any related record regardless of field type, but the question is about which field types can be summarized.)

question: Cloud Kicks (CK) increased its Salesforce development efforts so that it now has multiple custom development efforts happening in parallel. CK's developers and admins perform the customizations and have complained that working in one sandbox has led to many problems. They requested a solution in which they can work in at least 20 different sandboxes at once, that all start with the same base configuration and data. What should an app builder use to solve the problem?
optionC: Partial copy sandboxes
explanation: Partial Copy sandboxes include metadata plus a sample of production data (defined by a sandbox template) and are licensed in larger quantities than Full sandboxes, making them the practical choice when a team needs many parallel environments that all start from a common configuration and data baseline. Full Copy sandboxes are typically licensed in very small numbers and are too costly to use 20 in parallel. Reference: Salesforce Help — Sandbox Types.

question: Cloud Kicks (CK) wants to set up a custom child object to track gift cards issued to a customer. A key requirement is to track the total number of gift cards opened and gift cards issued on an Account. CK wants to permanently ensure the gift cards are unable to be moved across any other Account once it is created. On the gift card object, what type of field should be created to support this requirement?
optionC: Formula
explanation: Master-detail relationships (with 'Allow reparenting' unchecked, which is the default) prevent child records from being moved to another parent — satisfying the 'unable to be moved across any other Account' requirement. They also enable roll-up summary fields on the parent (Account) to count gift cards. A lookup relationship would allow the parent to be changed and does not support roll-up summaries.

question: At Universal Containers, all US Sales Reps should be able to view the US Team dashboard, however, only the US Sales Directors should be able to see the data in the component and view its source report. How can an app builder ensure the proper access is granted?
optionA: Make the US Sales Director the running user and share the dashboard folder with the role US Sales Rep.
optionB: Make the dashboard dynamic and give US Sales Reps the View My Team's Dashboard permission.
optionC: Share the dashboard folder with roles and subordinates of the US Sales Director and share the report folder with the role of US Sales Director.
optionD: Share the dashboard with the public group US Sales Reps and share the dashboard source reports folder with the US Sales Director profile.
explanation: Sharing the dashboard folder with 'Roles and Subordinates' of US Sales Director gives the reps access to view the dashboard. Sharing the report folder only with the US Sales Director role ensures that only directors can drill into the source report. Salesforce dashboards already enforce that to see component data, the running user must have access to the source report — by restricting the report folder, only directors can see the underlying numbers.

question: Cloud Kicks is redefining its entire business process to convert the Manager Notes field from a long text area field. The goal is to encourage managers to be more concise in their comments and stay at 255 characters or less. There is preexisting information in the Manager Notes field that often is well beyond the character limit. What would happen to any existing information if the app builder tries to convert a preexisting long text area field to text area?
optionA: Preexisting information will truncate to the first 255 characters.
optionB: Preexisting information will remain even if it was over 255 characters.
optionC: Preexisting information will cause an error message to pop up.
optionD: Preexisting information in the field will be completely lost.
explanation: When converting a Long Text Area to Text (max 255), Salesforce truncates existing values to the first 255 characters. The setup screen will warn that data may be truncated, but the conversion proceeds and excess characters are lost.

question: Which Opportunity standard fields are available to be configured directly? Choose 3 answers
optionC: Lead Source
explanation: Stage, Lead Source, and Type are standard picklist fields on Opportunity whose values can be edited directly. Forecast Category is derived automatically from the Stage value (configured via the Stage picklist's Forecast Category mapping, not directly). Probability is also automatically tied to Stage.

question: A new field has been added to the Applicant object that is part of an unmanaged package. A recruiter ran the Position with or without Applicants report and noticed that the new field was missing as an option to add as a column. How should an app builder troubleshoot this issue?
optionA: Adjust the field-level security to include in the report type.
optionB: Check Allow Reports for the Position and Applicant objects.
optionC: Add the field to the custom report type field layout.
optionD: Update the profile with the Manage Public Reports permission.
explanation: Custom report types do not automatically include new fields added to an object. The app builder must edit the custom report type and explicitly add the new field to its field layout to make it available as a column in reports built from that report type.

question: An app builder received a request to extend record access beyond the organization-wide defaults configured. Which two features satisfy this requirement? Choose 2 answers
optionC: Permission Set Groups
explanation: Sharing rules and manual sharing are the standard mechanisms for opening up record access beyond OWD. Sharing rules grant access to groups of users based on ownership or criteria. Manual sharing grants access to specific users or groups on individual records. Public groups are containers used inside sharing rules — they don't grant access on their own. Permission set groups bundle object/field/system permissions, not record-level access.

question: Universal Containers manages leads in a Lead qualification queue where sales reps can accept ownership of the Lead. Campaign members are required to have a sales owner. What validation rule should an app builder configure?
optionC: AND(ISNEW(), ISBLANK(Owner:User.Id))
explanation: Campaign members must have a sales (user) owner. The validation rule should fire whenever the Lead is owned by a queue (i.e., Owner:Queue.Id is not blank). NOT(ISBLANK(Owner:Queue.Id)) returns TRUE when the owner is a queue, blocking the save and forcing a sales rep to take ownership. Option C only blocks new records, not existing ones being edited while still owned by the queue.

question: An app builder wants to create a report to compare the number of support cases in each status (New, In-Progress, or Closed) and by priority (Critical, High, Medium, or Low). What solution should be used for the report?
optionC: Custom Report Type
explanation: Grouping rows by Status and columns by Priority (a matrix report / summary with two groupings) produces exactly the comparison the requirement describes. Bucket columns categorize values into custom groups, filters narrow data shown, and custom report types define which objects/fields are reportable — none of these compare counts across two dimensions on their own.

question: Sales reps want the ability to see who can view their account records and how the people have access. Which button should the app builder add to the Account page layout to enable this?
optionC: Sharing Hierarchy
explanation: The Sharing button on a record opens the Sharing Detail page, which lists every user with access and the reason for that access (owner, role hierarchy, sharing rule, manual share, team, etc.). Sharing Hierarchy shows role-hierarchy access only and is not an addable button on the page layout in the same way.

question: An app builder is tasked with adding key performance indicators on Account pages. They want to see a summary of the number of open Opportunities and the number of won Opportunities for each Account. Where should the app builder go to build these new rollups?
optionC: Lightning Object Creator
explanation: Roll-up summary fields are created on the parent (master) object. Account is the master in the Account–Opportunity relationship, so the roll-up summary fields counting open and won Opportunities are created on the Account object.

question: When an Opportunity Close Date is pushed more than 30 days, manager approval is required. An approval process is in place but reps frequently forget to submit for approval to run the process. How can an app builder ensure that these opportunities are submitted into the approval process?
optionA: Change the entry criteria on the approval process to criteria are met and lock the record on initial submission.
optionB: Use a validation rule and an email alert to the manager requesting approval.
optionC: Submit the record for approval from an automated process.
optionD: Give the manager the API Enabled permission to permit approval responses by email.
explanation: A record-triggered flow (or Process Builder) can call the 'Submit for Approval' action automatically when the Close Date is pushed by more than 30 days, removing the need for the rep to remember. Approval process entry criteria alone do not submit a record — submission is always an explicit action.

question: Universal Containers wants to deliver purchased containers to remote construction addresses. In these cases, the customers will supply UC with the coordinates to the location. What type of field should the app builder use to capture this information?
optionC: Text
explanation: A geolocation field stores latitude and longitude as a compound field and supports distance calculations in formulas, reports, and SOQL — the appropriate native field type for capturing coordinates.

question: DreamHouse Realty (DR) asks for some improvements in case management. They want to enforce process compliance so that cases are unable to be reverted to an earlier case status, and to ensure that certain fields are required when specific case criteria are met. Which solution should an app builder implement to meet these requirements?
optionA: Configure validation rules with help text.
optionB: Create dependent picklist fields and set them as required.
optionC: Use an approval process to check field criteria are met.
optionD: Make the fields required on the page layout.
explanation: Validation rules can compare PRIORVALUE(Status) to the new Status to block backward moves, and can conditionally require fields based on other field values (something page-layout 'Required' cannot do, since that makes the field required unconditionally). Both compliance requirements are met with validation rules.

question: To increase adoption, Universal Containers is proposing changes to its Salesforce data model to allow easier visibility for sales reps into key metrics. The proposal has three custom objects related to the Account object, one with a master-detail, and two that are not. Each of these objects has 15 fields they would like to summarize on the Account object. What are two considerations for this proposal? Choose 2 answers
optionA: Roll-up summaries allow MAX, MIN, SUM, COUNT, and AVG.
optionB: An object can have 20 object references.
optionC: An object can have 25 roll-up summaries.
optionD: Roll-up summaries are limited to master-detail relationships.
explanation: Two true considerations: (D) roll-up summary fields can only be created across master-detail relationships, so the two lookup-related objects cannot be summarized natively; (C) the standard limit is 25 roll-up summary fields per object (raisable to 40 by Salesforce). Option A is wrong because roll-up summaries support COUNT, SUM, MIN, MAX — not AVG.

question: Universal Containers (UC) has created a picklist field called Status on three separate custom objects. UC has a requirement to share the list of values for this field across each object. Which feature would an app builder use?
optionC: Dynamic Action
explanation: A Global Picklist Value Set (also called a Global Value Set) is a single, centrally managed list of picklist values that can be reused by picklist fields on multiple objects, ensuring consistency and one-place maintenance.

question: On the Account Lightning record page, users need to see ten fields and the ability to sort and wrap text on their Related Lists. What Related List type would the app builder select for the Related List Lightning component?
optionC: Related List — Single
explanation: The Enhanced List (Related Lists — Enhanced) component supports up to 10 columns, in-line text wrapping, and column sorting. Basic List displays only a few fields and lacks sort/wrap features.

question: Ursa Major Solar's service department gets requests for several types of services, such as installation, repair, and maintenance. Service managers need to be able to tell when maintenance was last done on an asset to help determine if they are meeting contract agreements, but the last maintenance date can be difficult to determine when there are many work orders related to the asset. They think it would be helpful to have a field auto-populated on the Asset record when a maintenance work order gets closed. What tool should an app builder recommend to help meet this requirement?
optionC: Apex Trigger
explanation: A record-triggered flow on Work Order can detect when status changes to Closed and the type is Maintenance, then update the related Asset's Last Maintenance Date field. Roll-up summary won't work because Asset–WorkOrder is a lookup, not master-detail. Flow is the declarative tool of choice.

question: Northern Trail Outfitters has two custom objects that are part of a master-detail relationship. What determines the ownership and sharing access of the detail record?
optionA: The default owner is set in the parent object's settings.
optionB: The Owner field on the Detail record.
optionC: The Owner field on the Master record.
optionD: The owner is set independently on the detail object's settings.
explanation: In a master-detail relationship, the detail record does not have its own Owner field. Ownership and sharing of the detail record are inherited from the master (parent) record's owner.

question: The Director of Marketing at Northern Trail Outfitters wants the app builder to create a formula field that tracks how many days have elapsed since a contact was sent a marketing communication. The director is only interested in whole units. Which function should be used to return a date for calculating the difference?
optionC: DATEVALUE()
explanation: TODAY() returns the current date as a Date value. Subtracting one Date from another yields a whole number of days, which is what the director wants. NOW() returns Date/Time and would produce fractional results.

question: Universal Containers is expecting impacts to operations due to increased demand. The executive team will be reaching out to current customers and want to see the number of open cases for the account and parent account. Which tool could an app builder use to display the number of open cases on the Account page?
optionC: Approval Process
explanation: Account-to-Case is a special standard relationship where Salesforce allows roll-up summary fields, so a roll-up summary counting open cases (with a filter on IsClosed = FALSE) is the simplest, no-code answer. (For Parent Account totals, a formula field that references the Parent Account's roll-up can be added.)

question: Universal Containers (UC) wants to delete data in several fields for 5,000 lead records. UC exports the selected record IDs and fields that need to have data deleted in a CSV file. Which two steps should an app builder suggest to meet these requirements? Choose 2 answers
optionB: Use Import Wizard to update Leads using the CSV file.
optionC: Select Insert Null Values in settings.
optionD: Use Data Loader to update Leads using the CSV file.
explanation: Data Loader has an 'Insert Null Values' setting that, when enabled, lets blank cells in the CSV overwrite existing field values with null — clearing the data. The Data Import Wizard ignores blank values, so it cannot be used to clear fields.

question: Cloud Kicks (CK) tracks the support level of its customers on the Account record page. CK wants to show a text notification on a Case record page when the related Account is a platinum-level customer. How could an app builder meet this requirement?
optionA: Add a Rich Text component to the Case Lightning page and set the component visibility to show when the Account Support Level is Platinum.
optionB: Create a text-only Visualforce page, drag the Visualforce component into the Case page layout, and set its visibility to show when the Account Support Level is Platinum.
optionC: Create a text-only Visualforce page, clone the Case page layout, drag the Visualforce component into the page, and assign the layout to platinum cases.
optionD: Clone the Case Lightning page, add a Rich Text area to the new page, and assign this page to platinum accounts.
explanation: In Lightning App Builder, a Rich Text component supports component visibility filters that can reference fields on the related parent (Account.Support_Level__c). This is the simplest declarative solution and avoids cloning pages or writing Visualforce.

question: Cloud Kicks (CK) keeps track of its shoe inventory in Salesforce. When an order's status is changed to Activated, the inventory for the ordered shoe is reduced. At that point, a SOAP web service on the CK website must be called so that the website is updated to display the correct inventory amount for the shoe. What should an app builder use to communicate to the CK web service when a shoe's inventory has changed?
optionC: Process Builder
explanation: Calling external services requires an asynchronous/after-save context. An after-save record-triggered flow can invoke an Apex action (or external service) that calls the SOAP endpoint. Before-save flows are for fast same-record updates and cannot make external callouts. Workflow rules can send Outbound Messages but those are SOAP-style notifications from Salesforce, not arbitrary web service calls.

question: Northern Trail Outfitters wants to change a master-detail relationship on Account to a lookup relationship with a custom object Park. The app builder tries to reconfigure this but is unable to do so. What could be causing this?
optionA: The Account is included in a flow process on the Park object.
optionB: The Park object needs at least one master-detail field for reporting.
optionC: The Account record includes Parks roll-up summary fields.
optionD: The Park records have existing formulas on the Account.
explanation: Converting a master-detail to a lookup requires removing all roll-up summary fields on the master object that summarize the detail object first, since lookups do not support roll-up summaries. Once the dependent roll-ups are deleted, the conversion can proceed.

question: Cloud Kicks has created a custom object called Interests which is joined to Accounts by way of a junction object called Account Interest. What is the impact to users attempting to view an Account and the associated Account Interest records if they are without read access to the Interest object?
optionA: Users will be able to view the Account Interest records and will have read-only access to the Interest records.
optionB: Users will be unable to view Account records that have a related Account Interest record.
optionC: Users will be unable to view the Account Interest records or the Interest records.
optionD: Users will be able to view the Account Interest record, but unable to view the field or any information relating back to the Interest record.
explanation: On a junction object with two master-detail relationships, the user must have at least Read access to BOTH parent objects to see any junction record. Without Read on Interest, the user cannot see any Account Interest junction record, nor any Interest record.

question: An app builder created multiple custom fields, page layouts, and reports in the sandbox and added them to a change set that was deployed to production. The reports were NOT deployed. What should the app builder do?
optionA: Move the reports to the Unfiled Public Reports folder and add them to a new change set.
optionB: Move the reports out of the Unfiled Public Reports folder into a public folder and add them to a new change set.
optionC: Recreate the reports in production. Reports are not supported in change sets.
optionD: Add the reports to an unmanaged package and install the unmanaged package into production.
explanation: Reports stored in personal folders or in the Unfiled Public Reports folder cannot be added to change sets. The reports must be moved into a shared public report folder before they can be packaged in a change set and deployed.

question: Universal Containers (UC) wants to test code against a subset of production data that is under 5 GB. Additionally, UC wants to refresh this sandbox every weekend. Which type of sandbox should be used to accomplish this?
optionC: Full Copy
explanation: Partial Copy sandboxes hold up to 5 GB of data based on a sandbox template and can be refreshed every 5 days, allowing weekly refreshes. Developer and Developer Pro sandboxes don't include production data automatically. Full Copy refreshes every 29 days, so it cannot be refreshed weekly.

question: Universal Containers has a custom object that holds over 100 fields. The app builder wants to break up the fields into separate tabs on the Lightning page. Which Lightning component is most appropriate to fulfill this requirement?
optionC: Field Section
explanation: The Tabs component on a Lightning record page lets the app builder organize content into separate, named tabs (e.g., Details, Related, Custom groupings) — the natural way to split 100+ fields into manageable groupings. The Accordion component is also valid for vertical collapsible sections, but the requirement specifically says 'separate tabs', which maps to the Tabs component.

question: Cloud Kicks wants to know the total value of all won Opportunities for Accounts and display it on the record. What type of summary should the app builder use in the roll-up summary field?
optionC: SUM
explanation: SUM aggregates the numeric values of a field across child records. To get the total Amount of all won Opportunities for an Account, use SUM on Opportunity.Amount with a filter for IsWon = TRUE.

question: Cloud Kicks has five years of sales data and would like to track when customers made their first purchase. How should an app builder use a roll-up summary to meet the requirements?
optionA: Create a new roll-up summary field called First Order Date, using type MIN on the Opportunity Close Date with a filter where IsWon = TRUE.
optionB: Create a new date field called First Order Date, create a workflow to set the date, and roll up the value with a filter where IsWon = TRUE.
optionC: Create a new roll-up summary field called First Order Date, using type SUM on Opportunity Close Date.
optionD: Create a new date field called First Order Date, then create a roll-up summary to update the field using type MIN.
explanation: MIN of Opportunity Close Date (filtered to IsWon = TRUE) returns the earliest won-opportunity date, which is the first purchase date. SUM cannot be applied to dates, and a separate workflow is unnecessary because roll-up summary fields directly compute the value.

question: The CFO of Cloud Kicks needs to sign off on any major shoe retail deal that has a discount of more than 30% before the deal can be closed. What feature would be used to handle this requirement?
optionC: Field Update
explanation: An approval process formalizes who must sign off, the criteria that trigger it, and what happens on approval/rejection. The CFO requirement (sign off on >30% discount before closing) is a textbook approval process. Email alert and field update are individual actions, not a sign-off mechanism.

question: Cloud Kicks works on an annual subscription model. When a sales rep marks an opportunity as Closed Won, a new opportunity should automatically be created for the renewal. The contracts team works outside of Salesforce but also needs to be notified about closed deals in order to initiate the contract process with the customer. Which automation solution would meet these requirements?
optionC: Validation Rule
explanation: A record-triggered flow on Opportunity (entry: status becomes Closed Won) can both create the renewal Opportunity record and send an email alert (or call an external service) to notify the contracts team — handling both requirements declaratively in one place.

question: Cloud Kicks wants to set up a new opportunity approval process and execute various action items based on the initial submission. Which three action types should an app builder use in the approval process? Choose 3 answers
optionC: Task
explanation: Approval processes support these action types: Email Alert, Task, Field Update, and Outbound Message. Of the listed options, Email Alert, Task, and Field Update are all standard approval-process actions. Outbound Message is also valid; 'Invocable Process Builder' is not a real action type.

question: An app builder has a custom component they want to make available on the utility bar, but the component is unavailable. How should the component be tagged?
optionB: For use in Lightning App Builder.
optionC: For use on the utility bar.
explanation: Custom Lightning components must declare lightning__UtilityBar as a target in their .js-meta.xml configuration to be available for the utility bar. Without that target, App Manager won't expose the component as a utility item.

question: UC has a requirement that an Opportunity should have a field showing the value of its associated Account's Billing State. This value should not change after the Opportunity has been created. What is the recommended solution to configure this automated behavior?
optionC: Workflow rule with Field Update (on create only)
explanation: A workflow rule (or record-triggered flow) that fires only when the record is created and copies Account.BillingState into a custom text field on the Opportunity captures the value at creation and never updates it again. A formula field would change as the Account changes, which violates the requirement.

question: Universal Containers is setting up Salesforce for the first time. Management wants the sales and marketing teams to have different navigation menus in the Salesforce mobile app. Which option is available to an app builder to satisfy the requirement?
optionA: Create sales and marketing profiles to ensure read access to different objects.
optionB: Create roles for sales and marketing and assign a custom homepage layout for each role.
optionC: Create separate Lightning apps with different navigation items for sales and marketing and assign each app to the corresponding profiles.
optionD: Create public groups for sales and marketing and create mobile navigation menus for each group.
explanation: In Lightning Experience (and the unified Salesforce mobile app), navigation in the mobile app comes from Lightning apps. Creating separate Lightning apps with different navigation items and assigning them to the appropriate profiles gives sales and marketing different mobile navigation. (Legacy 'Salesforce1 Navigation Menu' was replaced by Lightning app-based navigation.)

question: An app builder needs to deploy a new Account detail page layout from sandbox to production. Which three components should an app builder include in the change set to ensure it deploys successfully and visually as expected? Choose 3 answers
optionC: Custom actions
explanation: Page layouts, custom fields referenced on the layout, and any custom (quick) actions placed on the layout must all be included so the layout deploys successfully and looks the same in production. Profiles are not required to deploy a layout itself, and Lightning App Builder is a tool, not a deployable component.

question: The sales team receives a list of approximately 800 leads each morning from the marketing team. The marketing team does not know if any of the leads are currently in the pipeline and sends the entire list each morning. Which tool should be used to import these leads into Salesforce while preventing duplicates from being inserted?
optionC: Data Loader
explanation: The Data Import Wizard supports Lead, Contact, Account, and Solution imports natively and includes built-in duplicate matching (by name & email, etc.) to prevent duplicate Lead inserts. Data Loader does not include built-in dedupe logic.

question: An app builder wants to show Groups as the last navigation menu item in the mobile app. However, the app builder is unable to select Groups as one of the items on the drop-down menu. What could cause this?
optionA: Groups is available in the Recent section of the navigation menu.
optionB: Groups is included in the Smart Search Items but unavailable on the navigation menu.
optionC: Groups is unavailable in the selected list for the navigation menu.
optionD: Groups is available in the Chatter section of the navigation menu.
explanation: Groups is part of Chatter and appears in the Chatter section of the mobile navigation menu rather than as a standalone selectable item — which is why it doesn't appear in the items dropdown to add as the last menu item.

question: The app builder needs to change the data types of new custom fields. The app builder is not able to delete and recreate any of the fields, nor modify any Apex code. Which data type change will require the app builder to perform additional steps in order to retain existing functionality?
optionA: Changing the data type of a field used in an Apex class from Number to Text.
optionB: Changing the data type of a field used in a report from Text to Encrypted.
optionC: Changing the data type of a field used as an external ID from Number to Text.
optionD: Changing the data type of a field used in lead conversion from Number to Text.
explanation: Apex code is strongly typed; changing a referenced field from Number to Text will cause compile errors in any Apex class or trigger that uses it (variable assignments, arithmetic, comparisons). Since the app builder cannot modify Apex, additional steps are required. The other options don't break their respective features the same way.

question: An app builder notices several Accounts converted from Leads are missing information they expected to be caught via Account validation rules. What could be the source of this issue?
optionA: The Lead Settings option to require validation rules and triggers for converted leads is unchecked.
optionB: Account validation rules fail to validate on records converted from a lead.
optionC: The Lead Settings are allowing users to intentionally bypass validation rules.
optionD: Lead validation rules fail to validate on records when they are being converted.
explanation: Lead Settings includes a checkbox 'Require Validation for Converted Leads.' If it is unchecked, validation rules on Account, Contact, and Opportunity are skipped during lead conversion, allowing records that wouldn't otherwise pass validation to be created.

question: Cloud Kicks's management team frequently travels and wants to approve requests from their team on the go via Chatter. Where would an app builder enable this ability?
optionC: Chatter Settings
explanation: The 'Allow Approvals' option (Approvals in Chatter) is enabled in Setup > Chatter Settings. With it on, approval requests can be posted to Chatter and managers can approve/reject from the feed.

question: Universal Containers wants to match Opportunity data from Salesforce to the records in a financial database. What is required to configure an indirect lookup relationship in Salesforce between the Salesforce Opportunity records and those in a financial database?
optionC: External ID and Unique custom field on the parent (Salesforce) object
explanation: An indirect lookup relationship matches an external object's child records to a Salesforce parent object using a custom field on the parent that has both the External ID and Unique attributes. The external object's indirect-lookup field stores the matching key.

question: The CFO of Cloud Kicks needs a way for new vendors to accept terms on agreements for any new major retail store lease before the opportunity can be closed. Which feature should be used to handle this requirement?
optionC: Approval Process
explanation: An approval process can require vendor acceptance/sign-off as a step that must be completed before the opportunity is allowed to close. Email alert and dynamic action don't enforce a sign-off gate; validation rules can block save but don't model sign-off.

question: An app builder wants to deploy a new version of an autolaunched flow to production in an active state so that the new functionality is immediately available to users. What should the app builder take into consideration when planning the deployment?
optionA: Verify there is an Apex test that provides test coverage for the flow.
optionB: Grant user access to the flow.
optionC: Manually activate the flow after deployment.
optionD: Include the Process Builder calling the flow in the deployment.
explanation: Deploying an active flow (or any flow being activated) to production requires at least 75% Apex test coverage in the org just like any deployment that changes flow/process metadata. The app builder should ensure adequate test coverage exists prior to deployment.

question: Universal Containers (UC) has large data volumes and is nearing data storage limits. The planned solution is to archive historical data to reduce data storage in Salesforce; however, UC would still like to use reports, queries, and lookups on the archived information. Which two options could meet this requirement? Choose 2 answers
optionC: Related Objects
explanation: Big Objects store massive volumes of data within Salesforce in a way that doesn't count against standard data storage and supports async SOQL/queries. External Objects (via Salesforce Connect) reference data stored outside Salesforce in real time, also avoiding storage usage. Both support reporting and lookups (with some limitations).

question: Universal Containers would like to embed a chart of all related Opportunities, by stage, on the Account detail page. Which type of report should the app builder create to add to the Account page layout?
optionA: A summary report on the Opportunity object.
optionB: A summary report on the Account object.
optionC: A tabular report on the Account object.
optionD: A tabular report on the Opportunity object.
explanation: To embed a chart on a record page, the report must be a summary, matrix, or joined report (tabular reports cannot have charts) and must filter by the parent record. A summary report on Opportunity grouped by Stage produces the desired chart on the related Account.

question: Cloud Kicks (CK) wants to simultaneously delete a Supplier's record and all SupplierItem__c records if a partnership ends with a supplier. What solution could an app builder use to meet the requirement?
optionC: Hierarchical
explanation: Master-detail relationships cascade delete: when the parent (Supplier) is deleted, all detail records (SupplierItem__c) are automatically deleted. Lookup relationships do not cascade delete by default.

question: Due to the complexity of the Universal Containers sandbox release schedule and requirements, it is advised that change sets are used as often as possible to migrate from one environment to another. Which three common items can an app builder move when using a change set? Choose 3 answers
optionC: Custom object
explanation: Custom objects, Apex classes, and custom fields are all supported components in change sets. Web-to-Lead settings and standard fields (other than picklist values added to standard picklists in some cases) are not deployable via change sets.

question: The Service Manager provided the app builder with color code requirements for case age on open cases: New cases populate a green circle, day-old cases populate a yellow circle, three-day-old cases populate a red circle. How should an app builder implement this requirement?
optionC: Custom Button
explanation: A formula field using IMAGE() with a CASE() based on case age can render a green/yellow/red icon directly on list views and page layouts — a no-code solution that meets the requirement without any custom development.

question: Cloud Kicks wants to efficiently increase the company's adoption of Salesforce while simultaneously moving away from their reliance on spreadsheets. An app builder is given a spreadsheet that needs to be added to Salesforce. The object with fields needs to be created and the data inserted simultaneously. Which tool should be used?
optionC: Data Loader
explanation: Lightning Object Creator takes a spreadsheet (or Google Sheet/CSV), creates a new custom object with fields mapped from the columns, and imports the rows in a single guided flow. Import Wizard and Data Loader load data only into existing objects.

question: The marketing director is concerned that too many car parts were given away for free last year. Which functionality should be used to ensure all free parts receive the marketing director's sign-off?
optionC: Automated email message
explanation: An approval process formalizes the sign-off requirement: when a record meets the entry criteria (e.g., Price = 0 or Discount = 100%), it must be submitted for approval and the marketing director's approval is required before the record proceeds.

question: Universal Containers wants to display the real-time stock price for each account on the Account record page. How should an app builder implement this request?
optionA: Add a dynamic report to the page layout.
optionC: Install a solution from the AppExchange.
optionD: Build a flow that uses API calls.
explanation: Real-time stock prices come from external financial APIs. The simplest, declarative path for an app builder is to install a prebuilt component from the AppExchange that already handles the integration and embed it on the Account record page via Lightning App Builder.

question: Universal Containers has several new fields they've requested for the Opportunity Product object. What should an app builder be able to configure using a formula field?
optionA: A hyperlink to the parent Account of the parent Opportunity.
optionB: A Rich Text Area field that uses HTML to bold certain characters.
optionC: A combination of the Opportunity's Name and a Description field.
optionD: A mix of functions and concatenation of 10 Account fields and 10 Opportunity fields.
explanation: From Opportunity Product (OpportunityLineItem), formulas can traverse to the parent Opportunity and then to the parent Account, and the HYPERLINK() function can build a clickable URL to that grandparent Account record. Formula fields cannot output Rich Text/HTML formatting (B), and option D would likely exceed compiled-formula size limits.

question: AW Computing uses a private sharing model for opportunities. Whenever an opportunity with a type of Service Agreement is created, all users in the Service Manager role should be able to view the opportunity. Which tool should AW Computing use to accomplish this?
optionC: Apex sharing rules
explanation: Criteria-based sharing rules grant access based on field values on the record itself (e.g., Type = 'Service Agreement'), regardless of who owns it — exactly what's needed here. Owner-based rules share based on who owns the record, not record content.

question: Universal Containers uses a custom picklist field Account Region on the Account record. They want this region to be reflected on all related Contact records and stay in sync if the value of this field changes on the Account. How should an app builder meet this requirement?
optionA: Create a picklist field 'Account Region' on Contact and a workflow rule to update it when the Account field changes.
optionB: Create a formula field on Contact set to ISPICKVAL(Account.Account_Region__c).
optionC: Create a formula field on Contact set to TEXT(Account.Account_Region__c).
optionD: Create a text field 'Account Region' on Contact and a workflow rule to update it when the Account field changes.
explanation: A cross-object formula field with TEXT(Account.Account_Region__c) returns the current picklist text from the parent Account and stays in sync automatically with no automation required. ISPICKVAL returns a Boolean — wrong return type.

question: Northern Trail Outfitters uses a custom object to track travel requests. Rangers want to have automatic posts on a record whenever a travel request has been approved. Which feature should be used to accomplish this?
optionC: Feed tracking
explanation: Enabling Feed Tracking on the relevant object/fields (e.g., Status) causes Chatter to automatically post in the record's feed when tracked field values change — including 'Status changed to Approved' — without any custom automation.

question: How should an app builder configure access to a Contact's Twitter profile for Salesforce mobile app users?
optionA: Add a formula field to the Contact page layout.
optionB: Add an AppExchange Lightning component to the mobile app.
optionC: Add the Twitter component to mobile-view Lightning pages.
optionD: Add a Twitter quick action to the mobile navigation.
explanation: Salesforce includes a standard Twitter Lightning component that can be added to record pages (with a Phone form factor in Lightning App Builder) to surface a Contact's Twitter profile and recent tweets in the mobile app.

question: Universal Containers assigns system access via permission sets and permission set groups to ensure each user has proper access. One department with varying levels of support staff has five consistent permission sets they require in order to complete their duties. Some higher-level staff have additional permission sets that are only required for them. How should an app builder recommend assigning permission sets to users?
optionA: Use the Manage Assignments button to assign a permission set group and additional individual permission sets to each user.
optionB: Use the Manage Assignments button to assign each user with the same set of permission set groups and permission sets.
optionC: Use the Data Import Wizard to mass update the desired users with their full list of permission sets and permission set groups.
optionD: Use Data Loader to mass update the desired users with their full list of permission sets and permission set groups.
explanation: Bundle the five common permission sets into a permission set group and assign it to everyone in the department, then assign the additional individual permission sets to higher-level staff on top. This minimizes maintenance and reflects the intended use of permission set groups.

question: The services manager wants to make sure the team enters Case priority consistently. What feature can an app builder use to accomplish this?
optionC: Next Best Action
explanation: Path provides per-stage Key Fields and Guidance for Success on records, so users see (and can be required to fill out) the correct fields — including Priority — at each step of the case lifecycle, encouraging consistent data entry.

question: An app builder is creating a custom object called Testimonial__c and wants to connect Testimonial__c records with both the submitter's Contact record and Account record. If the Account is deleted, the Testimonial__c should also be deleted. If the Contact is deleted, but the Account remains, the Testimonial__c should remain. How should this be accomplished?
optionA: Make both the Contact and Account fields required on the Testimonial__c object and create lookup relationships from Testimonial__c to Contact and to Account.
optionB: Create a master-detail relationship from Testimonial__c to Account and a lookup relationship from Testimonial__c to Contact.
optionC: Make Testimonial__c a junction object between Account and Contact using master-detail relationships.
optionD: Create a lookup relationship from Testimonial__c to Account and a master-detail relationship from Testimonial__c to Contact.
explanation: Master-detail to Account gives the desired cascade-delete behavior (deleting the Account deletes its testimonials). Lookup to Contact lets the testimonial survive when the Contact is deleted. Reversing them (D) would cascade-delete with Contact instead.

question: Cloud Kicks has three types of customer support processes: Platinum, Diamond, and Bronze. The app builder created separate record types for each process on the Case object. The customer support team should be unable to create new cases with the Bronze record type. How should this requirement be met?
optionA: Update the organization-wide defaults to private for Case.
optionB: Update the support team profile to remove the Bronze record type assignment.
optionC: Create a permission set group for Case that includes Platinum and Diamond record types.
optionD: Make the record type hidden to support users; update sharing rules to private.
explanation: Record type assignments are controlled at the profile (and permission set) level. Removing Bronze from the support team profile's record type assignments stops them from selecting it on new Case creation, while keeping read access to existing Bronze records.

question: Universal Containers' sales reps can modify fields on an opportunity until it is closed. The sales operations team has access to modify the Post-Close Follow-up Date and Post-Close Follow-up Comments fields after the opportunity is closed. After the opportunity is closed, the rest of the fields are read-only. How should these requirements be met?
optionA: Use record types with field sets and restrict editing fields using field-level security.
optionB: Use field-level security on page layouts to restrict editing fields.
optionC: Use field-level security on page layouts with record types to restrict editing fields.
optionD: Use field-level security to mark fields as read-only on the Sales profile.
explanation: Use two record types (Open and Closed) with their own page layouts. On the Closed layout, mark all fields except the two follow-up fields as read-only. Combined with profile-based record type assignment and field-level security, this delivers exactly the differential editability described.

question: A Service Coordinator (SC) for Ursa Major Solar (UMS) does a final review of work orders owned by a technician for a specific region before the records are submitted for an invoice. Before closing out the work order, the SC needs to modify data or remove attachments that were added by mistake. The SC also needs access to any other related records owned by the technician. What solution would provide the required access, given a private data model?
optionA: Give the SC a permission set with the Modify All Data system permission.
optionB: Put the SC in the role hierarchy above the technicians whose work orders they review.
optionC: Create a workflow rule that updates records owned by technicians in that region with the SC.
optionD: Change Work Order access on the SC's profile to Modify All.
explanation: Placing the SC above the technicians in the role hierarchy automatically grants the SC read/edit access to all records owned by users below them, across all related objects — exactly the broad, regional supervisory access described — without resorting to org-wide Modify All Data.

question: The DreamHouse Realty (DR) service manager has asked for some improvements in case management to enforce process compliance so that cases are unable to be reverted to an earlier case status, and to ensure that certain fields are required when specific case criteria are met. What solution should an app builder implement to meet these requirements?
optionC: Validation Rules
explanation: Validation rules can compare PRIORVALUE(Status) to the new value to block reverting and can conditionally require fields based on other field values — both compliance requirements covered by one feature.

question: Cloud Kicks works on an annual subscription model. When a sales rep marks an opportunity as Closed Won, a new opportunity should automatically be created for the renewal. The contracts team works outside of Salesforce but also needs to be notified about closed deals in order to initiate the contract process with the customer. Which automation solution would meet these requirements?
optionC: Record-Triggered Flow
explanation: A record-triggered flow is the modern, recommended automation: it can both create the renewal Opportunity and send an email alert to the external contracts team when an Opportunity moves to Closed Won. Process Builder is being retired by Salesforce; flow is the preferred tool.

question: The case handling process at Universal Containers includes multiple steps including approvals, notifications, and field updates. To manage and evaluate all of these changes in a single save operation, an app builder wants to use Process Builder and the advanced option to let the process evaluate a record multiple times has been selected. Which two options should the app builder avoid to prevent recursion? Choose 2 answers
optionB: Setting a criteria node to 'No criteria — just execute the actions'
optionC: Invocable processes
explanation: When 'Recursion — Allow process to evaluate a record up to 5 times in a single save' is on, two patterns commonly cause runaway loops: (B) a criteria node set to 'No criteria — just execute the actions' will always re-fire because nothing gates it; (C) invocable processes called from another process can repeatedly trigger each other. ISCHANGED actually helps prevent recursion.

question: After utilizing the Lightning Object Creator to create a new object, its fields, and to insert all of the data, an app builder now needs to set up the Lightning record page. Which component should the app builder have on their Lightning record page to see all of the fields from the page layout?
optionC: Record Detail
explanation: The Record Detail standard Lightning component renders all sections and fields from the assigned page layout — the simplest way to expose the full layout on a Lightning record page.

question: The Convert button on Leads should NOT appear until the Lead Status picklist is set to Qualified. What should an app builder suggest to meet these requirements?
optionA: Picklist dependency, page layouts, record types
optionB: Custom button, validation rule, record types
optionC: Process Builder field update, quick action, record type
optionD: Page layouts, record types, dynamic forms / dynamic actions
explanation: The cleanest declarative approach: create separate record types (e.g., 'Unqualified' and 'Qualified') with their own page layouts (or use dynamic actions/forms with visibility rules) so the Convert action only appears when Status = Qualified. Custom buttons + validation rules (B) work but require replacing the standard convert flow.

question: Universal Containers has several large customers that sell their products through dealers. Each customer and dealer has an individual rep who works directly with UC, and each is billed separately. How can an app builder implement these requirements?
optionA: Create a single account record, add each rep as a contact, and create a custom dealer object.
optionB: Create both customer and dealer as accounts, add each rep as a contact on the corresponding account, and create an account hierarchy.
optionC: Create a single parent record, add each rep as a contact to the parent account, and add each dealer as a child record.
optionD: Create both customer and dealer as accounts, create account teams on each account, and associate the dealer records with the parent account.
explanation: Each entity that has its own bill and its own rep should be an Account. Creating customer and dealer accounts separately, attaching each rep as a Contact on its account, and using Account Hierarchy (Parent Account) to express the customer–dealer relationship is the standard pattern.

question: Universal Containers implemented an application process that uses custom objects Internships and Applications. The organization-wide default for Internships has been set to private and is the master in the master-detail relationship with Applications. The VP of HR wants to allow edit access to Applications to recruiters. How should an app builder configure the proper access?
optionA: Set the organization-wide default on the Applications object to Read/Write.
optionB: Add a sharing rule that grants the users Read/Write access to the Internship records.
optionC: Create a queue for the web applications and assign access to the users who will be editing the records.
optionD: Create a sharing rule that grants the users Read/Write access to the Application records.
explanation: Detail records inherit sharing from the master in a master-detail relationship — the detail object doesn't have its own OWD or sharing rules. To give recruiters Read/Write on Applications, share the parent Internship records with them via a sharing rule.

question: An app builder wants to add the option to 'Send New Email' from Leads, Contacts, and Accounts for users on mobile. What is the benefit of using global actions to accomplish this?
optionA: Global actions can be accessed from many places — record pages, the publisher menu, feeds, and Chatter groups.
optionB: Salesforce Lightning Component Library houses existing global actions prebuilt for use.
optionC: The global action's layout automatically clones the default page layout.
optionD: Global actions are record-specific and are available when searching that particular object.
explanation: Global actions are not tied to a specific object and can be surfaced in many places — global publisher, Chatter feed, groups, and the mobile navigation/utility bar — letting users send a new email from Leads, Contacts, or Accounts using the same action.

question: Universal Containers uses the Asset object to track products that are installed at customer locations. A new object, Asset Inventory, has been created to capture details about the asset. Which approach should the app builder take to show Asset Inventory as a related list on Asset?
optionA: Create a roll-up on Asset. Add the Asset Inventory related list to the Asset page layout.
optionB: Create a junction object to relate Asset Inventory and Asset. Add the Asset Inventory related list to the Asset page layout.
optionC: Create a lookup relationship on Asset Inventory to Asset. Add the Asset Inventory related list to the Asset page layout.
optionD: Create a master-detail relationship on Asset to Asset Inventory. Add the Asset Inventory related list to the Asset page layout.
explanation: A lookup (or master-detail) on the child object pointing to the parent is what produces a related list on the parent. A lookup is appropriate here unless cascade-delete and inherited sharing are required (in which case master-detail would also work). Note: master-detail must be created on the child object pointing to the parent — option D's wording is reversed.

question: Ursa Major Solar (UMS) uses a public sharing model for accounts. UMS would like to move to a more restrictive sharing model but wants the sales team to continue to have access to all account records with the sales record type. Which two actions should an app builder complete to implement this change? Choose 2 answers
optionC: Create a criteria-based sharing rule.
explanation: First, tighten the OWD on Account to Private (or Public Read Only). Then, create a criteria-based sharing rule that grants the sales team access to records where Record Type = Sales, restoring the access they need without opening up everything.

question: When an opportunity Close Date is delayed by more than 60 days, the manager and the VP of Sales must approve the change. How can this requirement be met? Choose 2 answers
optionA: Build an approval process that requires unanimous approval from the manager and VP of Sales.
optionB: Create a workflow rule that checks for Close Date less than 60 days and add an email alert.
optionC: Create a record-triggered flow that submits the record for an approval process.
optionD: Build a validation rule that does not allow a user to save the opportunity record.
explanation: (A) Build a multi-approver approval process requiring unanimous approval from manager and VP. (C) Use a record-triggered flow (or Process Builder) to detect when Close Date moves more than 60 days and automatically submit the record for that approval process so reps don't need to remember to submit.

question: Universal Containers expects impacts to operations due to increased demand. The executive team will reach out to current customers and wants to see the number of open cases for the Account and parent Account. What should an app builder use to display the number of open cases on the Account page?
optionC: Roll-up Summary
explanation: Account-Case is one of the few standard lookup relationships that supports roll-up summary fields. A roll-up summary on Account counting child Cases where IsClosed = FALSE produces the desired count without any code or automation.

question: DreamHouse Realty is rethinking its sandbox utilization strategy after acquiring Cloud Kicks. The Salesforce CoE already utilizes a partial and a full sandbox, which they refresh on their own regular schedules. Teams are expanding and have to begin each of their small projects in a sandbox before committing to the larger pool for collaborative testing while still keeping costs down. What type of sandbox should each team member use?
optionC: Developer Pro sandbox
explanation: Developer sandboxes are the lowest-cost, smallest sandboxes (200 MB), refreshable daily, intended for individual development work — exactly fitting 'each team member starts a small project before promoting to shared testing.' Full and Partial are heavier and reserved for the CoE's existing pool.

question: Cloud Kicks wants to start tracking how many shoe subscriptions have been sold for each shoe catalog. A master-detail relationship exists between the Subscription__c and the Shoe__c objects. Which type of field should an app builder create?
optionC: Master-detail relationship field
explanation: With a master-detail relationship in place, a roll-up summary field of type COUNT on the parent (Shoe__c) counting Subscription__c records gives an automatic, always-up-to-date subscription count.

question: What should an app builder consider when choosing a template for a new Lightning record page?
optionA: The template cannot be changed after the initial save.
optionB: To view the record page, users need View All Data permissions for the object.
optionC: The page structure will automatically adapt to the device being used to view the record page.
optionD: Select a new template for each type of device users use to view the record page.
explanation: Lightning page templates are responsive: their regions reflow for desktop, tablet, and phone form factors automatically. App builders don't pick a separate template per device.

question: An app builder wants to use Process Builder to automate some of the work being done by the sales team. What are three capabilities of Process Builder that can improve productivity? Choose 3 answers
optionC: Send an outbound message.
explanation: Process Builder supports email alerts, updating records (including related records), creating records (including child records), submitting for approval, posting to Chatter, calling Apex, and launching flows. It does NOT support deleting records or sending outbound messages directly.

question: Universal Containers uses a private sharing model on Accounts. User A and User B both own Accounts of their own and have both been sent a new account record in an email owned by User C to take a look at. User A is able to open and view the record but User B receives an insufficient privileges error. User A and User B have the same role in the role hierarchy. What are three reasons User A has access but User B is unable to access the record? Choose 3 answers
optionA: User A is on the same account team as User C.
optionB: User A and User B have different profiles.
optionC: User C has manually shared the record with User A.
optionD: User A was granted an additional permission set.
optionE: User A is in a public group that has access via a sharing rule.
explanation: All three are record-level sharing mechanisms that can grant User A access while leaving User B without it: account team membership (A), explicit manual sharing by the owner (C), and a sharing rule that targets a public group containing User A but not User B (E). Profiles and permission sets control object/system permissions, not record-level access.

question: Cloud Kicks has a sales rep who is stating that their Contact is unavailable for other users to see within Salesforce. In which three ways can an app builder troubleshoot this issue? Choose 3 answers
optionA: Create an Account sharing rule to give the users access to all records.
optionB: Confirm whether default organization-wide sharing settings provide access to the Account.
optionC: Review the Contact record and ensure it is linked to an Account.
optionD: Verify the users with the issue have access to the Contact object.
optionE: Create a new Contact and have the users try again.
explanation: Contact visibility is governed by (B) the OWD/sharing model on the parent Account, (C) whether the Contact has a parent Account at all (private contacts are only visible to owner), and (D) whether the affected users actually have Read on the Contact object via profile/permission set.

question: Sales reps at Universal Containers use Salesforce on their mobile devices. They want a way to add new contacts quickly and then follow up later to complete the additional information necessary. What mobile solution should an app builder recommend?
optionA: Customize the mobile menu to move Contacts to the top.
optionB: Build a global action to create Contacts.
optionC: Add a compact layout to Contacts.
optionD: Use Path and set predefined values.
explanation: A global Create-Contact quick action with only the essential fields on its action layout lets sales reps create a Contact from anywhere in the mobile app in a few taps, then return later to fill in the rest.

question: At Ursa Solar Major, only users with the Outer Planets profile need to see the Jupiter field on the Solar System object. How should the app builder satisfy this requirement?
optionC: Field-level security
explanation: Field-level security (FLS) controls which profiles/permission sets can see and edit a given field. Hide the Jupiter field for all profiles except Outer Planets to meet the requirement. Sharing rules control record-level access, not field-level visibility.

question: Universal Containers wants sales reps to get permission from their managers before deleting Opportunities. What can be used to meet these requirements?
optionA: Process Builder with Submit for Approval action.
optionB: Approval process with a triggered flow process.
optionC: Approval process with time-dependent workflow action.
explanation: Out of the box, deletion can't be gated by approval. The pattern is: a Process Builder (or record-triggered flow) submits the record for approval when reps flag it for deletion, and only after approval is the record actually deleted (e.g., by an action on final approval).

question: An app builder is preparing to deploy a new app from the sandbox to production using change sets. What two considerations should an app builder keep in mind during this process? Choose 2 answers
optionA: Salesforce Connect automatically establishes a link between environments.
optionB: Change sets do not include all components and may have to perform some changes manually.
optionC: A deployment connection between source and target orgs must be authorized before change sets can be sent.
optionD: Transactions will revert if the deployment errors.
explanation: (B) Not every metadata type is supported in change sets — some items (e.g., Web-to-Lead settings, certain custom settings rows) must be configured manually after deploy. (D) Change set deployments are atomic: if any component fails to deploy, the entire deployment is rolled back so the org is left in a consistent state.

question: An app builder at Cloud Kicks created a custom object and related fields in the Schema Builder. What next steps should the app builder take to ensure users can access the new object and fields?
optionA: Create a permission set for access to the object and fields.
optionB: Allow reporting for the object and fields.
optionC: Assign data types to the fields on the object.
optionD: Add the fields to the page layout on the object.
explanation: Schema Builder creates the metadata, but newly created custom objects and fields are not automatically visible to non-admin users. The app builder must grant object and field permissions via a profile or, preferably, a permission set so users can access them.

question: An app builder has downloaded a component from the AppExchange successfully; however, they are unable to add it to the Lightning home page. Which two reasons can be preventing the app builder from being able to add the custom component? Choose 2 answers
optionA: My Domain must be deployed to add custom components to the page with the App Builder.
optionB: A custom tab must be created to add custom components to the page with the App Builder.
optionC: The component requires a developer permission to add it to the page with the App Builder.
optionD: The component is tagged for record pages instead of home pages and is not showing up in the App Builder.
explanation: (A) My Domain must be deployed for custom Lightning components to appear in App Builder. (D) The component's targets in its meta config determine where it can be placed; if it only declares lightning__RecordPage and not lightning__HomePage, it won't appear when editing a Home page.

question: Cloud Kicks (CK) wants to quickly insert a list of over 60,000 net-new Accounts. The template based on CK's data model was used to populate the list. Which tool should be used?
optionC: Import Wizard
explanation: Data Loader handles up to 5 million records per load via Bulk API and supports Account inserts. The Data Import Wizard caps at 50,000 records per load and so cannot handle 60,000 in one go.

question: Universal Containers asked the app builder to ensure when an Account Type changes to 'Past-Customer' the Contacts directly related to that Account get an updated status of 'Re-Market'. Which automation should the app builder use to accomplish this task?
optionC: Validation rule
explanation: A record-triggered flow on Account (entry: Type changes to 'Past-Customer') can loop through related Contacts and update each one's status to 'Re-Market' — the canonical declarative pattern for cross-record updates triggered by parent changes.

question: The VP of Sales at Universal Containers has asked the app builder to let sales reps create opportunity records directly from the Account, with a number of fields pre-populated. Which feature should the app builder use to allow users to create the opportunity?
optionC: A custom button
explanation: An object-specific quick action 'Create Opportunity' on Account lets you define a tailored action layout and predefined field values, so reps can create the related Opportunity from the Account with the right fields prefilled.

question: When a deal is Closed Won, it has to be approved by the owner's manager prior to being added to the leaderboard for a quarterly sales competition. An opportunity is won on the last day of the quarter and the manager is on vacation. What is recommended to ensure all of the appropriate deals are reviewed and the leaderboard is up to date?
optionA: Forward the approval request to the manager's assistant.
optionB: Set up a delegated approver for the manager.
optionC: Have the manager log on and reassign the approval request.
optionD: Use Process Builder to assign a delegated approver.
explanation: Each user record has a Delegated Approver field. Configuring one for the vacationing manager allows the delegate to approve incoming requests on the manager's behalf — the standard Salesforce solution for OOO approval coverage.

question: Universal Containers (UC) has several large customers that sell their products through dealers. UC identifies and works with a single individual at each customer and at each dealer. Separate bills are sent to each customer and each dealer. These details need to be stored in a format that clearly displays the business entities and their appropriate representatives. How should an app builder implement these requirements?
optionA: Create a single parent record, add each rep as a contact to the parent account, and add each dealer as a child record.
optionB: Create both customer and dealer as accounts, create account teams on each account, and associate the dealer records with the parent account.
optionC: Create a single account record, add each rep as a contact, and create a custom dealer object.
optionD: Create both customer and dealer as accounts, add each rep as a contact on the corresponding account, and create an account hierarchy.
explanation: Each billed entity (customer, dealer) becomes its own Account. Each rep is a Contact on its respective Account. The customer–dealer relationship is expressed using Account Hierarchy via the Parent Account field — clearly displaying the business structure.

question: Universal Containers has 20 different workflows on the Opportunity object. To ensure that updates are processing properly for all field updates, UC has the 'Re-evaluate workflow rules after field change' checkbox checked. Recently after adding a new workflow, users have reported receiving errors about workflow limits. What should an app builder look at to address this?
optionA: Talk to a developer about Apex code issues.
optionB: Number of workflows per object limits.
optionC: Workflows that cause each other to fire back and forth recursively.
optionD: Workflows on other objects that are being re-triggered.
explanation: When the 'Re-evaluate workflow rules after field change' option is on, two workflows whose field updates trigger each other can keep firing until the platform's recursion/governor limits are hit, producing 'workflow limit' errors. This is the most common cause of the symptom.

question: DreamHouse Realty (DR) employees started using company-owned airplanes for work travel after Ursa Major Solar was acquired. DR executives want to automate the submission of travel request forms to enforce the internal policy. How should an app builder automate travel requests based on these criteria?
optionC: Approval process
explanation: An approval process is the natural fit for travel requests requiring policy-driven sign-off: define entry criteria, route to the appropriate approvers, and capture the approve/reject decision and audit trail.

question: The Universal Containers data manager has been complaining about the lack of data integrity on Contact records. Sales reps have not been filling out the Region field. The data manager wants the Region field filled out only for Contacts that are associated to Accounts that have been marked as 'High Priority' on the Customer Status field. What can the app builder do to fulfill this requirement?
optionA: Make the Region field required on Contact.
optionB: Create a validation rule on Contact.
optionC: Create a validation rule on Account.
optionD: Make the Customer Status field required on Account.
explanation: A validation rule on Contact such as AND(ISPICKVAL(Account.Customer_Status__c, 'High Priority'), ISBLANK(Region__c)) enforces conditional requirement based on the parent Account's Customer Status — something a layout-level Required setting cannot do.

question: AW Computing has a custom object for service plans. A service plan needs to be associated to one and only one contact. The support manager noticed if the wrong contact is associated, the reps are unable to change the contact. The app builder already confirmed the user has correct access to the field and there are no validations associated with the service plans. What could be causing the issue?
optionA: The Read Only radio button — Allows users with at least Read access to the master record to create, edit, or delete related detail records — is selected.
optionB: The Allow Reparenting checkbox — Child records can be reparented to other parent records after they are created — is unchecked.
optionC: The Read/Write radio button — Allows users with at least Read/Write access to the master record to create, edit, or delete related detail records — is selected.
optionD: The Allow Reparenting checkbox — Child records can be reparented to other parent records after they are created — is checked.
explanation: In a master-detail relationship, the Allow Reparenting option must be enabled to allow the parent of an existing detail record to be changed. With it unchecked (default), the lookup field on the detail record is effectively locked once set, exactly matching the symptom.

question: An app builder has deployed a change set from a sandbox to production. There is a long delay in the deployment. What can be causing the delay?
optionA: Profiles are included in the change set.
optionB: A field type change is included in the change set.
optionC: Dependent fields are included in the change set.
optionD: Roles are included in the change set.
explanation: Field type changes (e.g., Text to Number, Number to Text) require Salesforce to scan and convert every existing record's value for that field, plus revalidate dependent metadata (formulas, validation rules, Apex). On large data volumes this is the most common cause of long deployment times.

question: The app builder at Ursa Major Solar has just created a master-detail relationship between a parent object Galaxy__c and child object Star__c. What would be the effect of creating this type of relationship if users want to report on Galaxy__c with Star__c?
optionA: A Star__c report type with Galaxy__c as a field will be automatically created.
optionB: A new custom report type will need to be created for Star__c with lookup fields from Galaxy__c.
optionC: A Galaxy__c with Star__c report type will be automatically created.
optionD: A new custom report type will need to be created for Galaxy__c with Star__c.
explanation: When a master-detail relationship is created between two custom objects, Salesforce automatically generates a standard report type 'Parent with Child' (here, 'Galaxies with Stars'), so users can immediately report on parents joined with their children.

question: An app builder creates an Account validation rule on the Industry field that will throw an error if the length of the field is longer than 6 characters. Another app builder creates a workflow rule with a field update that sets the Industry field to 'Technology' whenever the Billing City field is set to 'San Francisco'. What will happen the next time a salesperson saves an Account with a Billing City of San Francisco?
optionA: The record will save and the Industry field will change to Technology.
optionB: The record will not save and the validation rule's error message will be displayed.
optionC: The record will not save and no error message will be displayed.
optionD: The record will save but the Industry field will not change to Technology.
explanation: On save, validation rules run after workflow field updates re-evaluate. The field update sets Industry to 'Technology' (10 chars), which then fails the length-≤6 validation rule, blocking the save and surfacing the validation rule's error message.

question: An app builder is creating a Lightning record page and has added Mobile & Lightning Actions to the page layout. What two components could be included on the Lightning record page to display the actions? Choose 2 answers
optionC: Activities
explanation: The Highlights Panel surfaces the top action buttons from the Mobile & Lightning Actions section of the page layout. The Path component also exposes layout actions associated with each step. Chatter and Activities show their own posting/logging actions, not the page-layout action list.

question: Which two places can an app builder go to see a list of available custom Lightning components in their org? Choose 2 answers
optionC: Lightning App Builder
explanation: Setup > Lightning Components lists all custom Lightning (Aura and LWC) components in the org. Lightning App Builder also surfaces them in the Components panel under Custom when editing a page.

question: Northern Trail Outfitters wants the field sales team to only see the accounts that they own. Separate North American and European marketing teams should only see accounts in their respective regions. The inside sales team needs to see all accounts in Salesforce. How can this be accomplished?
optionA: Set the Organization-Wide Default to Public for accounts. Create criteria-based sharing rules for each marketing team, and create an Inside Sales Team permission set with the View All setting for accounts.
optionB: Set the Organization-Wide Default to Public for accounts. Create profiles for each marketing team, and create an Inside Sales Team role that is at the top of the role hierarchy.
optionC: Set the Organization-Wide Default to Private for accounts. Create criteria-based sharing rules for each marketing team, and create an Inside Sales Team profile (or permission set) with the View All setting for accounts.
optionD: Set the Organization-Wide Default to Private for accounts. Create permission sets for each marketing team, and create an Inside Sales Team profile with the View All setting for accounts.
